2606.22780.title: Explainable AI for Mental Health Prediction in Drug-Affected Populations with Dragonfly Algorithm and GAN Oversampling
2606.22780.paper_summary: Mental illnesses among drug users are an increasing international issue, particularly in regions where early detection cannot be easily undertaken. The current literature tends to ignore the use of AI-based mental health analysis in drug users, and low quality of the class imbalance treatment, low interpretability, and optimal hyperparameter optimization can lower predictive quality and clinical utility. This study present a detailed, explainable machine learning (ML) model of multiclass mental health prediction, using a multidimensional data set of drug-affected persons. We combine hybrid PCA-Information Gain (PCA-IG) feature selection, Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-based oversampling, and Dragonfly Algorithm (DA)-optimized XGBoost to address some of the limitations of existing methods. The suggested framework is effective to work with high-dimensional categorical data, address the issue of class imbalance, and improve predictive performance due to intelligent hyperparameter tuning. The experimental findings show that the XGBoost model optimized using the DA, in combination with GAN-based oversampling, has an accuracy of 94.17% and a weighted F1-score of 93.80%, which is better than the traditional and baseline models. The behavioral, lifestyle, and health factors, particularly sleep quality, physical health, and emotional regulation, are strongly predictive of mental health, with demographic factors having little impact, as seen through feature analysis. SHAP-based explainable AI provides easy-to-understand, instance-level information, enhancing interpretability and trust in models to be used in clinical settings. The results indicate that this framework has the potential to generate valid mental health forecasting tools, which would facilitate early intervention and enhance the treatment of drug-influenced people.
2606.22780.paper_summary_zh: 精神疾病在藥物使用者中是一個日益嚴重的國際問題，尤其是在早期檢測難以進行的地區。當前文獻往往忽視了基於人工智慧的藥物使用者心理健康分析的應用，而低質量的類別不平衡處理、低可解釋性和最佳超參數優化可能會降低預測質量和臨床效用。本研究提出了一個詳細且可解釋的多類別心理健康預測機器學習（ML）模型，使用了一個受藥物影響的人的多維數據集。我們結合了混合主成分分析-信息增益（PCA-IG）特徵選擇、基於生成對抗網絡（GAN）的過採樣，以及經過蜻蜓算法（DA）優化的XGBoost，以解決現有方法的一些限制。所建議的框架能有效處理高維類別數據，解決類別不平衡問題，並因智能超參數調優而改善預測性能。實驗結果顯示，經過DA優化的XGBoost模型，結合基於GAN的過採樣，其準確率為94.17%，加權F1-score為93.80%，優於傳統和基準模型。行為、生活方式和健康因素，特別是睡眠質量、身體健康和情緒調節，對心理健康具有強預測性，而人口統計因素的影響則較小，這在特徵分析中得以顯示。基於SHAP的可解釋AI提供了易於理解的實例級信息，增強了模型在臨床環境中使用的可解釋性和信任度。結果表明，這一框架有潛力生成有效的心理健康預測工具，這將促進早期干預並改善受藥物影響者的治療。
2606.22780.author: Ahnaf Atef Choudhury et.al.
2606.22780.authors: Ahnaf Atef Choudhury, Shahriar Siddique Ayon, Md. Ebrahim Hossain, Abdullah Al Mamun
